title: Anisolotus longebracteatus
taxonomy: Anisolotus longebracteatus is classified in the family Lauraceae. Lauraceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the laurel family. This family is part of the order Laurales, which consists of 46 families of flowering plants. The Laurales order is within the clade Magnoliidae, a group of flowering plants that includes the most ancient and primitive flowering plant lineages. The Magnoliidae clade is part of the larger clade of angiosperms, which consists of all flowering plants.

intro: Anisolotus longebracteatus, commonly known as Long-bracted Anisolotus, is a plant species in the family Anisophyllaceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to protect against neurodegenerative diseases. The bioactive constituents of Anisolotus longebracteatus include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Anisolotus longebracteatus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, as well as being incorporated into formulas to support cognitive function and memory.
